REAL PROPERTY/HOUSING MOTION 501, DEFENDANT'S AMENDED MOTION TO STRIKE IS GRANTED AS SPECIFIED BELOW WITH LEAVE TO AMEND FOR PLAINTIFF TO ALLEGE FACTS SUPPORTING ENTITLEMENT TO ATTORNEY'S FEES UNDER THE TORT OF ANOTHER DOCTRINE. CURSORY ALLEGATIONS CONTAINED IN PARAGRAPH 12 IS NOT SUFFICIENT TO SUPPORT SUCH CLAIM, ESPECIALLY AS IT APPEARS TO APPLY TO ALL CAUSES OF ACTION ALLEGED IN THE COMPLAINT. THE COURT FURTHER NOTES THAT THE NOTICE OF MOTION SEEKS TO STRIKE THE ENTIRETY OF PARAGRAPH 4 OF THE PRAYER FOR RELIEF. THIS PRAYER REQUEST COSTS, INTER ALIA. REQUEST TO STRIKE PRAYER FOR COSTS IS NOT SUPPORTED BY POINTS AND AUTHORITIES. THEREFORE, THE MOTION IS GRANTED ONLY AS TO THE REQUEST FOR FEES, NOT AS TO THE REQUEST FOR COSTS. PREVAILING PARTY TO PREPARE ORDER IN COMPLIANCE WITH CRC 3.1312(B). JUDGE: CHARLES F. HAINES; CLERK: J. MURPHY; NOT REPORTED. (501)

DEFENDANT IRASEMA GUTIERREZ DEMURRER to 1ST Amended COMPLAINT is OVERRULED, 10 days to answer. Cause of action for declaratory relief is sufficiently stated. Prevailing party to prepare order in compliance with CRC3.1312(b). Judge Charles F. Haines, Clerk: N. Wong, Not reported (D501)

MOTION TO STRIKE
Off Calendar Sep-02-2020 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: APPEARANCES VIA COUTRT CALL:
Thomas Koster, Esq., (415)680-0023, appearing for Plaintiff Fish Tate.
Jacqueline Patton, Eeq., (415) 947-0797, appearing for Defendant Irasema Gutierrez.
Patricia Shnell, Esq., (415)701-1200, ext. 325, appearing for Defendant Felix Hernandez.

Not argued. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows: Defendant's Amended Motion to Strike is GRANTED as specified below with leave to amend for Plaintiff to allege facts supporting entitlement to attorney's fees under the Tort of Another Doctrine. cursory allegations contained in paragraph 12 is not sufficient to support such claim, especially as it appears to apply to all causes of action alleged in the Complaint. The Court further notes that the Notice of Motion seeks to strike the entirety of paragraph 4 of the Prayer for Relief. This prayer request costs, inter alia. Request to strike prayer for costs is not supported by points and authorities. Therefore, the motion is granted only as to the request for fees, not as to the request for costs. Prevailing party to prepare order in compliance with CRC 3.1312(b). Judge: Charles F. Haines; Clerk: J. Murphy; Not Reported. (501)
